authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the controlling federal Funeral Rule text for funeral-provider price disclosures, itemized statements, required-purchase explanations, embalming fee controls, direct-cremation casket rules, cash-advance disclosures, disclosure clarity, and retention of price lists and statements. These are core control obligations for funeral compliance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Funeral Rule obligation mapping and evidence readiness review
used_for: GPL, CPL, OBCPL, selected-services, and itemized-statement control mapping
required-purchase, cash-advance, direct-cremation, and embalming disclosure checks
record retention and inspection-readiness evidence
formal signoff refusal and regulator-facing boundary scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal Funeral Rule regulator. This official guidance is directly written for funeral providers and translates the rule into practical compliance workflow language for price lists, telephone price handling, selection statements, embalming disclosures, and records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Provider workflow translation and compliance handling
used_for: funeral-provider terminology and workflow stage design
evidence checklist language for arrangement and audit records
policy conflict and operational-practice scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC/NCHS publishes the national vital-statistics manual for funeral directors. It defines death-registration duties, demographic data collection, medical-certifier coordination, and filing dependencies that compliance review must treat as authoritative workflow evidence.
used_for: death-certificate and eDRS dependency handling
certifier, coroner, registrar, and permit owner routing
low-confidence blocker scenarios when vital-record facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's funeral and cemetery regulator publishes official public guidance on right to control disposition, funeral and cemetery licensing, GPL/CPL and itemized statements, embalming authorization, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery purchases, preneed issues, and complaint pathways. It is authoritative for California and demonstrates jurisdiction-sensitive state variance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: State-specific funeral, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, preneed, and complaint control review
used_for: right-to-control and jurisdiction ambiguity handling
cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, and preneed evidence expectations
complaint routing and state-regulator boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Committal Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, National Cemetery Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA National Cemetery Administration is the official source for national cemetery committal service process, burial-document handling, committal shelter limitations, and service timing. It constrains compliance support when veterans cemetery burial or committal service appears in the case.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Veterans cemetery committal dependency review and adjacent-owner routing
used_for: VA burial document dependency handling
national cemetery committal process boundaries
benefits and scheduling authority refusal scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1030 - Bloodborne pathogens
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA publishes the primary federal bloodborne-pathogens standard for occupational exposure controls, including exposure-control programs, training, protective equipment, housekeeping, medical follow-up, and recordkeeping that govern funeral-home safety evidence where employees may contact human remains or other potentially infectious materials.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Occupational exposure control and safety evidence review
used_for: exposure-control evidence expectations
training, PPE, incident, and recordkeeping control checks
safety-critical refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Application of provisions of the Bloodborne Pathogens standard to Funeral and Nursing Homes
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official interpretation applies bloodborne-pathogen obligations to funeral homes and human remains, making it directly authoritative for funeral-specific safety-boundary evidence and handoff rules when a requester asks compliance to authorize body handling or preparation.
used_for: licensed-owner and safety-owner routing
body-preparation refusal behavior
funeral-specific exposure-control terminology

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and jurisdiction-aware scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, identify location, jurisdiction, license context, business unit, case or location scope, required authority owners, and whether the lane can act without legal, licensed, regulator-facing, financial, or safety-critical authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map Funeral Rule, vital-record, state-regulator, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, veterans cemetery, occupational-safety, preneed, tenant-policy, and contract controls to evidence owners, systems of record, review cadence, and exception owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence assembly and system-of-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Collect and reconcile price lists, itemized statements, authorization records, death-registration references, disposition permits, cemetery or crematory confirmations, preneed files, complaint logs, exposure-control records, corrective actions, and audit workpapers.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness, gap, and conflict assessment
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether evidence is sufficient, missing, stale, contradictory, not owner-linked, or jurisdiction-sensitive before producing a control summary, readiness note, or blocker packet.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and owner escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, control failures, policy conflicts, operational shortcuts, right-to-control ambiguity, licensed-owner actions, preneed financial questions, and safety-critical issues to accountable owners with traceable context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Review or attestation preparation without formal signoff
workflow_stages.description: Prepare review-ready evidence bundles, obligation matrices, corrective-action summaries, and exception notes while refusing formal certification, legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or licensed signoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: General Price List, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, package price matrix, telephone or online price-disclosure logs, and location-specific price-list versions

statement of funeral goods and services selected, selected goods and services, cash-advance items, actual-charge update, required-purchase explanation, direct cremation alternative-container evidence, and itemization workpapers
embalming authorization or exception record, viewing or service request, licensed funeral director or embalmer owner, and preparation or safety escalation note

decedent case record, first-call notes, removal or transfer record, custody status, authorized arranger context, person authorized to direct disposition evidence, preneed instructions, and dispute notes
death-certificate demographic intake, medical certifier or coroner status, eDRS reference, registrar status, burial transit or disposition permit, and vital-record filing evidence
cemetery interment-right record, crematory or hydrolysis authorization, container requirement, chain-of-custody log, committal schedule, VA burial document reference, and destination confirmation
preneed contract, trust, insurance assignment, funding, cancellation, refund, assignment, and policy exception records
complaint records, regulator correspondence, internal audit workpapers, corrective-action plans, control attestations, exception approvals, remediation trackers, and review signoffs
OSHA exposure-control plan, bloodborne-pathogen training records, PPE and sharps-control evidence, incident or exposure logs, housekeeping records, and safety owner notes
systems_of_record: family-service CRM or arrangement management system
price-list, package, POS, invoicing, and billing system
electronic death-registration system or vital-record portal
cemetery management, interment-right, and committal scheduling system
preneed trust, insurance, assignment, and funding ledger
complaint, incident, corrective-action, and regulatory correspondence tracker
occupational-safety, training, exposure-control, and incident management system
audit, compliance monitoring, policy, and control repository
task, exception, approval, and orchestrator workflow system

allowed: Classify funeral compliance requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request and validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, systems-of-record references, and evidence owners before acting.

Map funeral, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, death-registration, preneed, veterans cemetery, occupational-safety, tenant-policy, and public-source obligations to controls, evidence, cadence, and owner routing.
Prepare evidence-backed control matrices, readiness summaries, exception notes, remediation escalation packets, and review packages without formal certification.
escalate: Required tenant facts, jurisdiction, facility context, license context, case records, source context, record locations, or authority owners are missing and would change the answer.
Public sources, jurisdiction rules, cemetery or crematory requirements, preneed documents, tenant policy, or operational practice materially conflict.

Right-to-control, family authority, licensed funeral directing, embalming, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, vital-record, preneed financial, benefits, legal, regulator-facing, or safety-critical ownership is required.
A control failure, missing evidence, repeated operational shortcut, complaint, or corrective action requires accountable owner resolution.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal compliance certification, legal signoff, or policy override.
Requests to authorize embalming, body preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, burial, transportation, committal, or safety-critical release outside licensed or authorized owners.
Requests to decide preneed contract enforceability, refund liability, insurance assignment, VA benefits eligibility, price exception, financing exception, or estate-payment responsibility.
Requests to mark controls, cases, locations, audits, complaints, or corrective actions complete when required evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or owner-unassigned.

domain_failure_modes: Treating funeral compliance as generic enterprise compliance and omitting GPL, CPL, OBCPL, itemized statement, cash-advance, required-purchase, direct-cremation, embalming, and retention evidence.
Universalizing one state regulator's right-to-control, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, preneed, or complaint rules across all tenant jurisdictions.
Marking a case or control complete even though death-registration, certifier, disposition permit, custody, cemetery, crematory, or committal evidence is missing or contradictory.
Normalizing operational shortcuts such as closing cases before signed statements, price-list evidence, or complaint and corrective-action records are in the system of record.
Resolving legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal certification, preneed financial disputes, benefit eligibility, or licensed-owner judgment inside the lane.
Treating embalming, body preparation, remains handling, cremation, hydrolysis, or transfer requests as compliance paperwork instead of licensed and safety-sensitive work.
Ignoring cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, or VA national cemetery requirements when they affect required-purchase disclosures, destination confirmations, scheduling, or service constraints.
Producing an evidence packet without traceable source systems, accountable evidence owners, review cadence, exception status, and tenant-specific assumptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific right-to-control, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery, preneed, death-registration, and complaint rules vary materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated pack treats jurisdiction, tenant policy, facility requirements, licensed-owner authority, and state-regulator context as mandatory runtime facts. It escalates rather than guessing when those facts change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems may use different names for price-list versions, signed statements, eDRS references, disposition permits, cemetery or crematory confirmations, complaints, and corrective actions.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant-specific system names, record locations, owner maps, and approval matrices before case-specific compliance handling. The pack defines source-of-truth classes and refusal behavior when mappings are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public baseline and tenant policy may impose different review cadence or evidence timing.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records tenant stricter controls, preserves the public baseline for comparison, and escalates conflicts to compliance or legal owners.

notes: The source set intentionally mixes primary Funeral Rule regulation, official FTC workflow guidance, CDC/NCHS vital-record workflow, official state funeral and cemetery regulator guidance, VA national cemetery operating guidance, and OSHA safety standards so it covers policy, workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because jurisdiction and tenant variance is converted into explicit prerequisite, escalation, or refusal handling.
